# Bridge to Polygon
> Bridge to Polygon covers self-custodied transfers of crypto assets from Ethereum or other networks to Polygon PoS, plus the routes, fees, timing, and risks involved.

## What is Bridge to Polygon
Bridging to Polygon moves an asset’s usable representation between separate blockchains rather than swapping it on one chain. Polygon PoS is an EVM-compatible Ethereum sidechain with its own consensus, gas token, and chain ID. Its official PoS bridge transfers mapped assets between Ethereum and Polygon without relying on market liquidity; third-party bridges may instead use liquidity pools, relayers, or other verification systems. An independent informational dashboard compares these routes without holding private keys or operating the underlying bridge.

## How it works
1. Select the source network, Polygon PoS as the destination, an asset, amount, and recipient; then review the route and estimated costs.
2. Connect a self-custody wallet and verify every network, token contract, approval, and destination address before signing.
3. On the official Ethereum-to-Polygon route, bridge contracts lock the source asset on Ethereum and issue its mapped representation on Polygon; native POL deposits produce native POL on Polygon.
4. Returning through the PoS bridge burns the Polygon representation, waits for checkpoint inclusion, and requires an Ethereum claim transaction to unlock the escrowed asset.
5. Third-party routes use their own liquidity and message-validation mechanisms, so execution, settlement, fees, and trust assumptions differ.

## Key facts
- Polygon PoS mainnet uses chain ID `137` and POL for gas.
- Ethereum mainnet uses chain ID `1` and ETH for gas.
- Polygon Amoy testnet uses chain ID `80002`.
- The official PoS bridge connects Ethereum and Polygon PoS; it does not directly bridge Polygon PoS to Polygon zkEVM.
- Mapped ERC-20, ERC-721, and ERC-1155 assets are supported by the native bridge contracts.
- The dashboard is non-custodial, but bridge contracts may escrow assets while representations circulate elsewhere.
- PoS withdrawals rely on Polygon validator checkpoints submitted to Ethereum and on upgradeable contracts governed through multisignature controls.
- Checkpoints are normally submitted roughly every 30 minutes; total arrival time also depends on confirmations, congestion, route design, and any required claim.
- Bridge transactions are irreversible once confirmed.

## Common questions
**Is bridging to Polygon safe?**  
No bridge is free of smart-contract, validator, governance, network, or user-error risk. Verify the domain, token mapping, route, approvals, and recipient, and consider a small test transfer.

**Who controls my funds?**  
The dashboard never needs a seed phrase or private key. You authorize transactions from your wallet, while the selected bridge’s contracts may lock or pool assets.

**What fees will I pay?**  
Costs can include source-chain gas, token approval, bridge or liquidity-provider fees, slippage, destination execution, and an Ethereum claim transaction. Review the complete route quote before signing.

**How long does it take?**  
Ethereum-to-Polygon deposits complete after the required source confirmation and bridge processing. Native Polygon-to-Ethereum withdrawals must reach a checkpoint before the claim; third-party routes can have different timing.

**Which assets and source chains are supported?**  
The native PoS bridge supports Ethereum and officially mapped assets. Other source chains and tokens depend on the third-party provider selected by the route.

**How do I start?**  
Use a compatible self-custody wallet, keep the source network’s gas token, choose Polygon PoS chain ID `137`, confirm the destination token, and test with a small amount.

## App
- Dashboard: https://bridge-to-polygon.github.io/
- App: https://bridge-to-polygon.github.io/dashboard/

## Official resources
Polygon Portal: https://portal.polygon.technology/
Polygon Portal Guide: https://docs.polygon.technology/tools/wallets/portal
PoS Bridge Overview: https://docs.polygon.technology/pos/how-to/bridging/ethereum-polygon/portal-ui
Mapped Tokens: https://docs.polygon.technology/pos/reference/mapped-tokens
Polygon PoS Network Details: https://docs.polygon.technology/pos/reference/rpc-endpoints
PoS Bridge Contracts: https://github.com/0xPolygon/pos-portal
Polygon Security Reports: https://docs.polygon.technology/tools/security/reports
Polygon Official X: https://x.com/0xPolygon

PolygonScan Explorer: https://polygonscan.com/
Etherscan Explorer: https://etherscan.io/
CoinGecko POL: https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/polygon

ERC-721 Standard: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-721
ERC-1155 Standard: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-1155
